spec_match.rationale: The builder correctly formats the combined cross-origin, integrity, and referrer-policy URL request modifier syntax across multiple CSS contexts.

interactive.rationale: The builder features checkboxes, text fields, select dropdowns, context tabs, and a copy-to-clipboard button that update the output block dynamically.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned perfectly with the parent description of an interactive tool to construct and view generated modifiers.

edge_cases.rationale: The tool now runs the generated rule through CSSStyleSheet.insertRule() on every render and surfaces accepted and syntax-error states for valid, malformed, and context-specific CSS.

references.rationale: The concept page now has direct ChromeStatus, CSS Values Level 5, and MDN url() links.

design_system.rationale: Perfect design system conformance with appropriate font scaling, borders, and button structures.

summary: DevTools MCP reproduced the original gap at http://localhost:3000/v150/css-url-request-modifiers/modifier-builder/?verify=before-parser-validation: the builder updated generated CSS but had no parser-validation nodes and no direct reference links. The repaired page was tested at /v150/css-url-request-modifiers/modifier-builder/?verify=after-parser-validation-copy-fix in Chrome 148: background-image and @font-face generated rules with cross-origin(), integrity(), and referrer-policy() showed an Accepted badge, a malformed selector and @import modifier combination showed a Syntax Error badge with the CSSStyleSheet.insertRule() exception, all three context tabs updated the output, direct ChromeStatus/spec/MDN links were present, and the page had no console errors or failed network requests. The feature conformance route ran at /v150/css-url-request-modifiers/conformance/?verify=modifier-builder-parser-validation with 6 PASS / 4 FAIL / 10 TOTAL; the CSS.supports modifier assertions failed in Chrome 148 while server-side resource checks and strict bogus-modifier rejection passed cleanly.
